frase_original: All your THOUGHTS, are either an 'investment' to your well-being or a 'cost' to your well- being! Are your investments growing or the costs are too high..Think about it !

texto: Comparison kills...There will always be someone ahead of you and someone behind you in life...When you see someone behind you...feel blessed for you are ahead of them...When you see someone ahead of you...feel inspired for your own growth...Compare yourself to YOU of yesterday, if that's what you like to do...so you can become better today !
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Abha Maryada Banerjee: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “Comparison kills...There will always be someone ahead of you and someone behind you in life...Whe…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que lideranca, growth, leadership pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e leadership.

texto: We must teach our children to get 'EXCITED' about life and the World around them,instead of 'painting' their minds with OUR potrayal of 'how' the World is...children learn from 'emulating' their elders,not by being told...which asks US to 'live' that excitement.If we do not live that way then LEARN it and pass it on to our children..We will be doing them a HUGE FAVOR!
explicacao: Orientação direta de Abha Maryada Banerjee sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “We must teach our children to get 'EXCITED' about life and the World around them,instead of 'pain…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a educacao, children, learning, útil em momentos de learning e vida.

texto: When you learn to say 'NO', you activate the faculty of 'CHOICE' in your mind ! Some of the times you are the chosen one..at other times you CHOOSE for yourself..and saying NO is a sign that one has graduated into becoming a wise CHOICEMAKER ......
explicacao: Citação de Abha Maryada Banerjee sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “When you learn to say 'NO', you activate the faculty of 'CHOICE' in your mind ! Some of the times…” articula sabedoria, choices, free-will em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como free-will e leadership.
